_about: Hand-authored PSA grading rubric consumed by the grading agent. Values verified 2026-07-01 against psacard.com/gradingstandards and psacard.com/resources/lingo. Certainty markers: [C]=stated on psacard.com, [L]=reputable secondary source, [G]=inference. Grades 5-and-below and the weakest-factor cap rule are [L] — verify verbatim before treating as authoritative.
_scaleNote: PSA issues whole grades 1-10 plus half-grades 1.5-8.5. There is NO 9.5 and no 10.5. [C]

gradeDefinitions.10: [C] A virtually perfect card. Four perfectly sharp corners, sharp focus, full original gloss, free of staining of any kind. An allowance may be made for a slight printing imperfection if it doesn't impair overall appeal. Centering must be approx 55/45 or better on the front and 75/25 or better on the reverse.
gradeDefinitions.9: [C] A superb condition card that exhibits only ONE of the following minor flaws: a very slight wax stain on reverse, a minor printing imperfection, or slightly off-white borders.
gradeDefinitions.8.5: [L] Between NM-MT 8 and Mint 9 — a strong 8 approaching 9.
gradeDefinitions.8: [C] Appears Mint 9 at first glance but on close inspection may exhibit: a very slight wax stain on reverse, slightest fraying at one or two corners, a minor printing imperfection, and/or slightly off-white borders.
gradeDefinitions.7: [C] Slight surface wear visible on close inspection. There may be slight fraying on some corners.
gradeDefinitions.6: [C] May have visible surface wear or a printing defect that does not detract from overall appeal. Corners may have slightly graduated fraying.
gradeDefinitions.5: [L] Minor rounding of corners; noticeable surface wear and/or printing defects; some loss of original gloss.
gradeDefinitions.4: [L] Corners may be slightly rounded; noticeable but modest surface wear; possible light scuffing or scratches.
gradeDefinitions.3: [L] Some rounding of corners, not extreme; obvious surface wear; possible light creasing.
gradeDefinitions.2: [L] Significant/obvious wear — scratching, scuffing, or creases.
gradeDefinitions.1.5: [L] Considerable wear; may appear rounded/soft with creasing but still fully intact.
gradeDefinitions.1: [L] Extreme wear. Need not be fully intact; eye appeal has nearly vanished.

centering._measure: Border/margin ratio of the image within the card, worst axis (left-right or top-bottom). Front tolerance is stricter than back. [C] for 10-5.
centering.belowFive: [L] At grade 4 and below, centering is generally not the limiting factor (wear dominates). A card worse than ~90/10 that otherwise qualifies is typically dropped a grade or given the OC qualifier rather than held to a hard ratio.

corners._measure: Sharpness vs. wear of all four corners, front and back.
corners.9: Corners essentially sharp; at most one negligible touch of wear. [L]
corners.8: Slightest fraying at one or two corners. [C]
corners.5: Minor rounding of corners begins to be noticeable. [L]
corners.3: Rounding of corners noticeable but not extreme. [L]

edges._measure: Condition of the four card edges — chipping, whitening, roughness, notching. Best assessed with a slight tilt to see the edge face; a flat-on photo under-reports edge whitening.
edges.10: Clean, sharp edges with no chipping or whitening. [G from 10 definition]
edges.9: Edges essentially clean; at most trivial whitening. [L]
edges.8: Minor edge wear/whitening visible on close inspection. [L]
edges.6: Visible edge wear that doesn't detract from overall appeal. [L]

surface._measure: Print focus/quality, original gloss, and defects: scratches, scuffing, print lines/dots, staining (wax/gum), indentations, and creases/wrinkles. Fine scratches and print lines are only reliably visible under raking/grazing light — a flat, even-light photo hides them (see surfaceCeiling).
surface.10: Sharp focus, full original gloss, free of staining of any kind. Allowance for a slight printing imperfection only if it doesn't impair appeal. [C]
surface.9: At most one minor flaw: a very slight wax stain on reverse, a minor printing imperfection, or slightly off-white borders. [C]
surface.8: Full gloss; at most a very slight wax stain and/or a minor printing imperfection. [C/L]
surface.7: Slight surface wear visible on close inspection; gloss largely intact. [C]
surface.6: Visible surface wear or a printing defect that does not detract from overall appeal. [C]
surface.5: Noticeable surface wear and/or printing defects; some loss of original gloss. [L]
surface.4: Noticeable surface wear; possible light scuffing or scratches. [L]
surface.3: Obvious surface wear; possible light creasing. [L]
surface.1: Extreme surface wear; eye appeal nearly gone. May include heavy creasing. [L]

overallRule: [L] PSA publishes ONE overall grade and NO printed subgrades. There is no published numeric formula — grading is holistic — but in practice the overall is effectively CAPPED BY THE WEAKEST of the four attributes: a single serious flaw (rounded corner, crease, heavy whitening) limits the grade regardless of the other three. It is NOT an average. When a card meets a grade in three attributes but fails one (most often centering), it is dropped to the next grade down, or may keep the higher grade WITH a qualifier (see qualifiers). Treat 'weakest attribute caps the overall' as the reconciliation rule.
surfaceCeiling: IMPORTANT capture limitation: unless the input explicitly includes a raking/grazing-light photo, surface defects (fine scratches, print lines, hairlines) may be INVISIBLE in even, flat lighting. When grading surface from flat-light-only input, treat the surface score as a CEILING / best-case: never award a top surface score with high confidence, phrase surface reasoning as 'no defects visible under standard lighting; hidden scratches possible', and lower confidence for the surface factor and the overall. Do not claim a clean surface you cannot actually see.

qualifiers.meaning: Off-Center — centering outside the threshold for the assigned grade.
Staining — manufacturing (e.g. wax) or later substance exposure.
Print Defect — printing-process artifacts (stray dots/lines not part of the design).
Out of Focus — image-clarity issues from production printing/focus.
Marks — writing, ink, or anything resembling writing on the card.
Miscut — factory cut abnormal; extreme border imbalance or image cut off.

_qualifierRule: [C/L] A qualifier means the card would have received that grade (or higher) if not for one specified flaw (e.g. 'NM-MT 8 OC'). If the submitter does NOT elect an optional qualifier, that same flaw instead lowers the straight numeric grade. MK/MC always appear when present.

noGrade._policy: [C] PSA will NOT assign a numeric grade to cards with evidence of trimming, re-coloring, restoration, cleaning, pressing, or added gloss, or of questionable authenticity, or that fail minimum-size specs. Such cards get an 'N' reason code and/or an Authentic (Authentic-Altered) designation.

codes.N1: Evidence of Trimming — hooked/unnaturally sharp/inconsistent/wavy edges.
codes.N2: Evidence of Restoration — paper stock built up (e.g. corners rebuilt).
codes.N3: Evidence of Recoloration — color artificially improved.
codes.N5: Altered Stock — restoration, crease/wrinkle pressing, scratch removal, or enhanced gloss.
codes.N6: Minimum Size Requirement — significantly undersized vs. factory spec (implies trimming).
codes.N7: Evidence of Cleaning — whitener on borders or solvent used to remove stains.
codes.N8: Miscut — factory cut abnormal for the issue.
codes.N9: Don't Grade — issue outside PSA's graded scope (oversized/obscure).

eyeAppeal: [C] Not a separately scored attribute but explicitly invoked: a slight printing imperfection is allowed at 10 only 'if it doesn't impair the overall appeal', and PR 1 is defined by eye appeal having 'nearly vanished'.
